Who We Are

We Build the Bridge.
Then Walk Across It With You.

Zoshani LLC is a private investment and technology transfer facilitation firm based in Minneapolis, Minnesota. We work at the intersection of African development priorities and the capital, technology, and expertise of advanced economies.

We structure deals, build partnerships, and stay engaged from the first conversation to the final execution.

The most durable outcomes come from enterprises that are commercially viable, not dependent on aid.

About Zoshani

Built to Build.
Not to Advise and Leave.

Zoshani designs and delivers transformational infrastructure and enterprise programmes that mobilise development finance while generating commercial returns. We specialise in public-private partnerships that align United States and other advanced economy strategic interests with the development needs of emerging markets.

Our work creates sustainable economic ecosystems — not interventions that depend on aid. Our experience spans development finance, agribusiness, energy, manufacturing, mining, infrastructure, and digital enterprise.

The most durable outcomes come from enterprises that are commercially viable, not dependent on aid.
Our Philosophy

Growth Begins Within

Africa does not lack intelligence, resources, or ambition. What it has lacked is access to the systems, capital, and technology that convert those assets into prosperity.

Zoshani builds those connections. We connect African governments and entrepreneurs with tools that advanced economies have already built — and that emerging markets have every right to access.

Our Goal

Lasting Capacity, Not Dependency

We connect African governments and entrepreneurs with financing, technology, partnerships, and expertise that create lasting industrial capacity rather than dependency. The goal of every engagement is a capability that remains — one that the community owns and operates long after Zoshani has moved on.

Africa is not a future opportunity. Africa is a present opportunity.
Our Foundation

Four Brand Pillars

◈
Commercial Strength

Every project we build is designed to stand on its own commercial viability — not on the generosity of donors or the continuation of aid.

◈
Development Impact

We measure success by the economic transformation we create in the communities we serve — jobs, income, and industrial capacity that persists.

◈
Long-Term Capacity

The goal is not a transaction. The goal is a capability that remains — owned and operated by the people it was built to serve.

◈
Execution That Lasts

We stay present from the first conversation to the final execution. We do not write reports and walk away. We build.

Leadership

The People Who Build

RONNIE NTAMBI
Founder & Chief Executive Officer

Ronnie Ntambi has spent nearly three decades building the bridge between African development priorities and the capital institutions of the West. His expertise spans African economics, development finance, and enterprise building across East Africa, West Africa, Europe, and the United States.

He founded Zoshani on the conviction that Africa's most transformative projects deserve the same quality of financial architecture as any deal on Wall Street. That conviction has driven every engagement Zoshani has taken on.

"We know where every door is. Our job is to make sure our clients walk through the right one."
What We Do

We Build the Bridge.
Then Walk Across It With You.

Zoshani connects entrepreneurs and governments in emerging economies with capital, technology, and strategic partners in advanced economies — from the first conversation to the final execution.

The Problem We Solve

Most Projects Never Find the Door

The United States and Europe have built extraordinary development finance instruments — DFC, EXIM Bank, USTDA, EIB, KfW. These institutions exist specifically to fund African infrastructure and enterprise. Most African projects never access this capital.

Not because they are not viable. Because they never find the door.

We know where every door is. And we walk through it with you.
Our Approach

Present from Day One
to Final Execution

Zoshani does not hand you a report and leave. We stay engaged through every phase — from opportunity identification to partnership formation, capital access, technology transfer, and ongoing execution. Our measure of success is a project that stands on its own.

i
Opportunity Identification
Analysis · Evaluation · Alignment
We identify and evaluate opportunities that align with African development priorities and the commercial interests of international partners. Every engagement begins with rigorous analysis — not assumptions.
ii
Partnership Facilitation
Missions · Feasibility · Site Visits
We organise fact-finding missions, feasibility studies, site visits, and project planning engagements. The work of building real partnerships requires presence. We show up.
iii
Financing & Capital Access
DFC · EXIM · USTDA · EIB · JBIC
We navigate United States and European development finance on behalf of African project sponsors. We understand the instruments, the officers, and the documentation required to unlock this capital. Most African projects never access it because they never find the door. We know where every door is.
iv
Technology Transfer
Equipment · Licensing · Training
We source equipment, license technology, train personnel, and oversee execution from design to completion. The goal is not a transaction. The goal is a capability that remains — one that the community owns and operates long after Zoshani has moved on.
v
Entrepreneur & Partner Identification
Matching · Scaling · Access
We identify high-potential entrepreneurs and match them with the partners and institutions they need to scale. Africa's most capable builders often lack only one thing: access. We provide it.
Who We Serve

Governments. Entrepreneurs. Global Businesses.

African Governments

Ministries and state agencies seeking to structure industrial investment, attract development capital, and build lasting economic infrastructure. We understand the public sector — and we work within it without losing commercial discipline.

Private Sector in Africa

Entrepreneurs and businesses with viable projects that need capital, technology, or international partnerships to reach their potential. We find the missing pieces and put them in place.

Private Sector in Advanced Economies

Companies in the United States, Europe, and Asia seeking structured, supported entry into African markets — including Japanese institutions with financing pathways through JBIC and NEXI.

Development Finance Institutions

We serve as a qualified intermediary — preparing project sponsors and coordinating the documentation and due diligence required before capital is committed.

Africa is not a future opportunity. Africa is a present opportunity.

Industries

Every Sector We Engage Is Grounded in Active Work

Agribusiness
Food Systems & Rural Enterprise

ASRII in Uganda is a fully integrated small animal farming system with a feed mill, processing infrastructure, organic by-product lines, and export channels.

Mining & Minerals
From Extraction to Production

Our Artisan Industrialization Initiative converts raw mineral wealth into certified, export-ready finished goods through women-led manufacturing enterprises.

Energy
Renewable Energy Finance

We facilitate renewable energy projects across Africa with financing from United States and European development institutions — DFC, EXIM, JBIC, NEXI.

Manufacturing
Industrial Capacity Building

We connect African manufacturers with technology providers and structure equipment financing through EXIM Bank and DFC, enabling industrial capacity that is locally owned.

Infrastructure
Build · Train · Deploy · Mentor

VACIDA embeds workforce development directly into live infrastructure projects. Infrastructure delivery becomes an education system. Capital investment becomes human capital.

Digital Enterprise
Mobile-First Platforms

INOZ is a mobile-first event services marketplace. MYZYK is a music rights platform enabling artists to earn royalties from public performances across East Africa.

Hospitality & Beverages
Farm to Market

Awakofi sources premium Ugandan coffee roasted in Germany. Watu Krafts is a craft brewery with 50 planned sites and contracts with 1,800+ smallholder farmers.

Portfolio

Commercially Engineered.
Community Benefiting.
Independent of Aid.

A portfolio of active ventures built on commercial strength and real economic value for the communities they serve. Every project is designed to create capacity, generate income, and remain viable long after the initial investment.

Agribusiness Uganda Active Development
ASRII
Alliance for Sustainable Rural Incomes Initiative

ASRII is a fully integrated small animal farming enterprise designed for peri-urban and rural communities. The programme includes a feed mill, processing infrastructure, organic by-product lines, and export channels. Phase 1 structures 5,000 farms across three districts, generating $11,436 per farm per year — creating sustainable rural economies at scale.

Government of Uganda mandate · $32.6M Phase 1 capex · 5,000 farms · 3 districts · Full vertical integration

Digital Marketplace East Africa Launching Q2 2026
INOZ
Mobile-First Event Services Marketplace

INOZ connects event organisers with verified service providers across Nairobi and Kampala. Built mobile-first for East Africa, INOZ brings structure, trust, and efficiency to a sector that has long operated informally. The platform creates income opportunities for thousands of small service providers while giving event organisers the tools for flawless execution.

Mobile-first · Django + DRF · M-Pesa, MTN, Airtel · AI contract engine · Tagline: Flawless Flow

Music Technology East Africa Pilot Phase
MYZYK
Music Rights & Revenue Platform

MYZYK places solar-powered units in entertainment venues across East Africa. For the first time, artists can earn royalties from public performances of their music. MYZYK strengthens the creative economy by ensuring that creators receive fair compensation for their work — building an infrastructure of dignity for African artists.

Solar-powered · Music rights · Royalty distribution · Venue network · East Africa

Coffee Uganda & Germany Development
AWAKOFI
Direct Trade Coffee

Awakofi sources premium coffee from Luwero, Paidha, and the Rwenzori region of Uganda. Beans are roasted in Hagen, Germany, creating a transparent farm-to-cup value chain that benefits farmers while delivering exceptional quality to global markets. Awakofi makes the story of the farmer the story of the coffee.

Luwero · Paidha · Rwenzori · Roasted in Hagen, Germany · QR farm-to-cup traceability

Craft Brewing East Africa Development
WATU KRAFTS
Craft Brewery Initiative

Watu Krafts is a craft brewery initiative with fifty planned sites across East Africa. The venture uses a triple-grain brewing model — barley, sorghum, and millet — and maintains direct contracts with more than 1,800 smallholder farmers. Watu Krafts strengthens local agriculture while building a modern beverage industry with deep community roots.

50 sites · 1,800+ smallholder farmers · Triple-grain model · €230M investment · 10,952 jobs

Mining & Manufacturing East & West Africa Structuring Phase
ARTISAN
Artisan Industrialization Initiative

This initiative transforms raw mineral wealth into certified, export-ready finished goods through women-led manufacturing enterprises and regional Centres of Excellence across Liberia, DRC, Tanzania, and Uganda. The goal is to shift value creation from extraction to production — allowing African communities to capture a far greater share of global mineral value.

Women-led · 40 Centres of Excellence · $60M capex · US technology · 6,000 artisan businesses

Infrastructure East Africa Initiative Development
VACIDA
Vault Africa Construction & Infrastructure Delivery Action

VACIDA embeds workforce development directly into live infrastructure projects. Using the Build-Train-Deploy-Mentor model, VACIDA turns every project into an education system. Infrastructure delivery becomes a classroom. Capital investment becomes human capital formation. The result is durable infrastructure and a trained workforce capable of building the next generation of national assets.

Build-Train-Deploy-Mentor · Workforce development · Live infrastructure projects · East Africa

Let's Build

Let Us Build Something Real.

Whether you represent a government ministry, a development finance institution, a technology company, or an entrepreneur with a project that needs structure and capital — Zoshani is the partner that makes complex deals happen.

Get In Touch

The Right Conversation Changes Everything

We work with a select number of partners and projects each year. If you have a serious project and a genuine commitment to execution, we want to hear from you.
